declared.title: Assessing the Impact of the 2026 Internet Shutdown on Uganda's Digital Economy
declared.selection_rationale: Single-country CIPESA brief on the same January 13-18, 2026 Uganda election-period internet shutdown documented by this corpus's existing OONI entry -- but from a distinct angle: quantified economic losses (UGX 59.7 billion / USD 16 million via the Cost of Internet Shutdown Tool), sector-by-sector business impact (e-commerce, ride-hailing, fintech/mobile money, digital health, government services), and the ongoing High Court litigation challenging the shutdown's legality. Confirmed by fetching and reading the full PDF -- genuinely substantive, not a repackaged press release. This is the real event that originally motivated ADR-0006's window extension to 2026; this document is the corpus's second entry documenting it (after OONI's), each contributing a materially different kind of evidence about the same event rather than duplicating one another. Publication date: PDF's own text states "March, 2026"; the CIPESA blog post announcing it was published 2026-04-01 -- used the PDF's own internal date per this corpus's standing preference for a document's own stated date over its announcement date, consistent with how ADR-0004's reproducibility-citation convention treats this class of discrepancy.
